keyPoints: 1. Apply 2D Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) to the input image.
2. Define the frequency mask for noise reduction, typically a rectangular mask or a Gaussian mask.
3. Multiply the frequency mask with the DFT of the input image to separate noise from signal.
4. Apply inverse DFT to get the filtered image.
5. Repeat the process to achieve optimal reduction of periodic noise.
6. Implement FIR filters for improved performance and reduced artifacts.
7. Compare and select the best filter (e.g., Butterworth) based on visual inspection.
8. Combine the frequency domain filtered image with the original image to restore the original image.
